description: This figure presents comprehensive feature engineering analysis for machine learning prediction of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel microhardness. The first composite panel includes: (a) a heatmap displaying feature selection results for hardness prediction with color-coded matrices showing correlation patterns; (b) a Pearson correlation matrix using colored circles (blue indicating negative, red indicating positive correlations) between compositional and physical features; (c) a bar chart ranking feature importance, where shear modulus (G), difference in cohesive energy (δ_Ec), and local cohesive energy mismatch (D_Ec) emerge as dominant predictors; and (d) partially readable supplementary data. The second panel plots cross-validation scores against numbers of features, showing performance stabilization beyond three features, thereby validating the optimal feature subset selection. These visualizations demonstrate that solid solution strengthening theory-based parameters (G, δ_Ec, D_Ec) identified through recursive feature elimination and exhaustive screening are critical for accurate hardness prediction (R²=0.89), supporting the integration of physical metallurgy principles into the SVR model.
